comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with this child (S3 Thread ref + source mode through the code-reading gates)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S4 30b3, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5). This child is the gate-threading / re-rooting slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. Empty G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no attested gate reads the working tree; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the PRIMARY OWNER of the re-rooting half: its AC1 (each of the five gates accepts ref+source; attested reads the snapshot dir, NEVER the server working tree), its AC4 (server's own checked-out branch no longer affects attested verdicts; the wrong-branch false-negative regression test passes), its AC5 (repo_root only locates the object DB; verified code is the snapshot). The faithful-materialization half (byte-match committed tree, export-ignore, LFS/submodule) is S1 (6d56 AC1/AC2). Heart of this child. Clean.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 record+sign+in-toto) with the immutable-SHA basis from S1. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child: it threads the ref and points the gate at the pinned-SHA snapshot but does not sign (its Scope: "Out: the signing rules (S4)"). Not an absence (S4 covers signing; this child's AC4 supplies the snapshot-only basis that makes reproducibility achievable).
- Parent AC3 (local mode reads in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the "reads in-place checkout" half (its AC2: local reads the in-place checkout, dirty allowed, and is flagged unsigned); S4 owns the "cannot sign" enforcement half (its AC2: a local/dirty run never emits a signature). Clean producer/enforcer split (this child marks-unsigned, S4 bars-signing); no double-implementation, no uncovered half.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; no worktrees; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered by S1 (atomic population, no-worktree, no index.lock/config.lock contention) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (its Scope: "Out: the snapshot/cache internals (S1/S2)"); claims none. Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2 (sole owner). Explicitly out of this child's scope (S1/S2). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises; its AC5) + S6 (documents; its AC2). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child; the coalesced fetch is S1's step. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (implementation; its AC4) + S6 (docs; its AC5). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #3 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot of the project at a pinned SHA — NEVER the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent use ...; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; unsigned local preserves verify-before-push") decomposes onto AC1-AC3. The "NEVER the server's mutable checkout" clause is precisely this child's core deliverable: its AC1/AC4/AC5 re-root every gate off cwd/repo_root onto the snapshot and prove it via the wrong-branch regression test that motivates the epic. The "client-specified" surfacing is S5; "signed/reproducible/verified_at_sha" is S4 atop the SHA basis this child threads; "unsigned local preserves verify-before-push" is reinforced by this child's AC2 (in-place dirty read, flagged unsigned). No uncovered obligation introduced.

Note: this child's "What"/Scope explicitly delegates to named consumers — signing rules to S4, CLI/MCP surface schemas to S5, snapshot/cache internals to S1/S2. Each delegated obligation is claimed by the named sibling (S4 30b3; S5 b7fa; S1 6d56; S2 70c7) — covered-by-named-consumer, not absences.

Coverage-pairing note (clean for G3): this child (S3) and S5 (b7fa) both name ref+source on the SAME five tool surfaces (review_plan/verify_completion/review_ticket/review_code/scan_spec). S3 threads the parameters into the gate dispatch and re-roots the file tools; S5 exposes them on the CLI/MCP schema with defaults. Producer/surface split, each scoped out of the other (S3: "Out: ... CLI/MCP surface schemas (S5)"; S5: "Out: the underlying behavior (S1-S4)"). No double-ownership, no coverage gap.

Note (scope, not a G3 finding): the existing G4 review on sibling S4 (30b3) flags a seam/dependency-gap around routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes() / claim-gate freshness re-check — a seam between S3 and S4 that neither lists attest.py in file_impact. That is a consistency (G4) concern about WHO threads the snapshot handle into the plan-review hashing path, NOT a G3 union-coverage gap: the corresponding parent obligation (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, and the ref-coherence clause) is still CLAIMED by a child (this child re-roots the gate code root; S4 claims claim-gate ref-coherence in its AC4). G3 union-coverage of the parent criteria is therefore complete; the WHO-threads-it question is left to the G4 channel.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the gate-threading slice (ref+source on all five gates, attested snapshot re-rooting, local in-place unsigned read, repo_root-as-object-DB-only, wrong-branch regression) of the parent criteria — it is the primary owner of the re-rooting half of parent AC1 and the in-place-read half of AC3; no parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PLAN-REVIEW TRIAGE (verdict PASS; 10 advisories). Accepted: (1) add ACs asserting the ref/source DEFAULTS (ref=origin/main, source=attested); (2) add an AC confirming all three re-rooting touchpoints (gate_ops, completion.py, operations._assemble_context) consume the snapshot handle, not just the interface; (3) state the expand-contract / back-out story for changing all five gates' code-root behavior (defaults mitigate, but make it explicit); (4) specify consumer tolerance when the snapshot dir is absent/stale/being GC'd (ENOENT -> re-materialize, per S2); (5) strengthen the AC4 regression oracle: assert the attested verdict is identical regardless of the server's checked-out branch (a behavioral contract, NOT an output snapshot/change-detector); (6) clarify repo_root's role in local mode; (7) ADR cross-ref (shared with S4).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S3 fa49: Thread ref + source mode through the code-reading gates)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache+janitor, S4 30b3 attested signing, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). This child is the gate-threading / re-rooting slice; the union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; this child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server's working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, with THIS child as the PRIMARY OWNER of the re-rooting half: its AC1 (each of the five gates accepts ref+source; attested reads the snapshot dir, never the server's working tree), its AC3 (all three dispatch touchpoints — gate_ops, completion.py, operations._assemble_context — CONSUME the snapshot handle, not just the interface signature), its AC6 (a gate tolerates an absent/being-GC'd snapshot by re-materializing, never reading a partial tree), and its AC7 (regression test asserts the attested verdict is IDENTICAL regardless of the server's checked-out branch — the behavioral contract reproducing this session's wrong-branch false-negative). S1 owns the faithful-materialization half (its AC1/AC2). Heart of this child. Clean.
- Parent AC2 (signed verdict records verified_at_sha + reproducible over identical content) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 record+sign+in-toto) atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. THIS child THREADS the ref + points the gate at the pinned snapshot that S4 signs over; its AC7 (identical verdict regardless of checked-out branch) directly reinforces reproducibility. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the signing act itself (this child's Scope: "Out: signing rules (S4)"). Not an absence (S4 covers signing).
- Parent AC3 (local source mode reads the in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict) — covered SPLIT: THIS child owns the "reads in-place checkout" half (its AC4: local mode reads the in-place checkout, dirty allowed, flagged unsigned, repo_root IS the read root; attested uses repo_root only to locate the object DB); S4 (30b3 AC3) owns the "cannot sign" bar. This child explicitly marks the run unsigned and notes "S4 bars signing" — clean reader/enforcer split, no uncovered half.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; no worktrees; atomic population; delete-on-last-close) — FULLY covered by S1 (atomic population, no-worktree, no index.lock/config.lock contention) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close). THIS child contributes the CONSUMER-side tolerance (its AC6: ENOENT -> miss -> re-materialize, never a partial tree) that makes eviction-under-an-open-handle harmless from the gate's perspective. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the population/eviction mechanism. Not an absence.
- Parent AC5 (snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under pressure without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered by S2 (sole owner). THIS child's AC6 (absent/being-GC'd snapshot tolerance) is the gate-side complement that lets reclamation proceed without disrupting a reader. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the reclaim mechanism (this child's Scope: "Out: snapshot/cache internals (S1/S2)"). Not an absence.
- Parent AC6 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered by S1 (raises; its AC5) + S6 (documents; its AC1/AC2). The fetch is S1's coalesced step; this child claims none. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC7 (portable: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered by S1 (implementation; its AC4) + S6 (docs; its AC5). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (this child's Scope: "Out: snapshot/cache internals (S1/S2)"). Not an absence.
- Parent AC8 (all six child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all six children (this child is story #3 of 6 in the roster) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Parent out-of-scope behavioral collapse (covered, not an absence): the parent's "code-review gets a single repo copy at a single ref ... like every other operation (no base+head pair)" is owned at the BEHAVIOR level by THIS child (its AC5: code-review uses a single snapshot at a single ref, no base+head pair). The complementary back-compat/expand-contract default (existing callers passing neither param get ref=origin/main, source=attested) and consumer-tolerance are also this child's own ACs (AC2 documented/configurable defaults; AC6 stale tolerance). (The SURFACE-side retirement of the pre-existing base/head/--diff-file controls is a standing G4 seam between S3 and S5 — a consistency concern about WHO retires the surface controls, not a G3 union-coverage gap; the single-ref code-review obligation is CLAIMED by a child here.)

Success-criteria narrative ("every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot of the project at a pinned SHA — NEVER the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent use ...; signed verdicts reproducible and bound to verified_at_sha; the unsigned local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3. The "NEVER the server's mutable checkout" clause is precisely this child's core deliverable: re-rooting the gate file tools / cfg.repo_path / pai_tools root onto the snapshot dir across all three dispatch touchpoints, with the AC7 regression test as the behavioral proof that the wrong-branch false-negative this epic exists to kill cannot recur. The "client-specified" surfacing is S5; the "immutable/reproducible/pinned-SHA" basis is S1; the "signed/verified_at_sha" act is S4; the "unsigned local preserves verify-before-push" reader half is this child (AC4), the signing-bar half is S4. No uncovered obligation introduced.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child's Scope explicitly delegates signing rules to S4 (30b3), CLI/MCP surface schemas to S5 (b7fa), snapshot/cache internals to S1/S2 (6d56/70c7), and the architecture ADR (authored in S2, cross-referenced here). Each delegated obligation is claimed by the named sibling. Covered-by-named-consumer.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4 seam, where THIS child re-roots only "the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path)" and lists completion.py/pai_tools.py/mcp_server.py (NOT attest.py) in file_impact — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the handle into the attestation hashing path, not a G3 union-coverage gap. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, and the ref-coherence clause) remain CLAIMED by children (this child's AC1/AC3/AC7 and S4's AC5), so the union covers them. G3 union-coverage of the parent criteria is complete.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the gate-threading / re-rooting slice (ref+source on all five gates; attested reads the snapshot dir across all three dispatch touchpoints; local in-place read flagged unsigned; back-compat origin/main+attested defaults that are configurable not hardcoded; consumer absent/GC'd-snapshot tolerance; single-ref code-review; the wrong-branch-invariance regression test) of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of parent AC1's re-rooting half and the "reads in-place checkout" half of AC3. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S3 fa49: Thread ref + source mode through the code-reading gates); audited against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster]

VERDICT: EMPTY G3 findings list. 4-bucket audit of this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE roster (S1 6d56 materialize core, S2 70c7 cache read/populate, S2b b73c janitor reclamation, S3 fa49 THIS gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). The union of the eight children covers every parent criterion; no uncovered parent criterion is attributable to this pairing.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS child's role + bucket):
- AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly, with THIS child as PRIMARY OWNER of the re-rooting half: its AC1 (each of the five gates accepts ref+source; attested reads the snapshot dir, never the server's working tree), AC3 (all three dispatch touchpoints — gate_ops, completion.py, operations._assemble_context — CONSUME the snapshot handle, not just the interface signature), AC6 (a gate tolerates an absent/being-GC'd snapshot by re-materializing — ENOENT -> miss — never reading a partial tree), AC7 (regression test asserts the attested verdict is IDENTICAL regardless of the server's checked-out branch — the behavioral contract reproducing this session's wrong-branch false-negative). The faithful-materialization half is S1 (6d56 AC1/AC2). Heart of this child. Clean.
- AC2 (signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY by S4 (30b3 AC1/AC2) atop S1's immutable-SHA basis. THIS child THREADS the ref + points the gate at the pinned-SHA snapshot S4 signs over; its AC7 (identical verdict regardless of checked-out branch) reinforces reproducibility. Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the signing act itself (this child's Scope: "Out: signing rules (S4)"). Not an absence.
- AC3 (local source mode reads the in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out restoring prior in-place read behavior) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child owns the "reads in-place checkout" half (its AC4: local mode reads the in-place checkout, dirty allowed, flagged unsigned, repo_root IS the read root; attested uses repo_root only to locate the object DB) AND the "documented back-out" half (its advisory AC "source=local documented as the rollback to in-place reads"; its What back-compat bullet). S4 (30b3 AC4) owns the "cannot sign" enforcement half. Clean reader+back-out / enforcer split; no uncovered half.
- AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; the snapshot cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark (S2) WITHOUT disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY by S1 (atomic/no-worktree population, no index.lock/config.lock contention) + S2 (single-flight, delete-on-last-close) + S2b (janitor reclamation under the watermark). THIS child contributes the CONSUMER-side complement (its AC6: ENOENT -> miss -> re-materialize, never a partial tree) that lets eviction-under-an-open-handle / GC stay harmless from the gate's perspective. Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. the population/eviction mechanism (this child's Scope: "Out: snapshot/cache internals (S1/S2)"). Not an absence.
- AC5 (private-repo fetch failure surfaces a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY by S1 (raises; its AC5) + S6 (documents; its AC1/AC2). The fetch is S1's coalesced step; this child claims none. Bucket STRUCTURAL. Not an absence.
- AC6 (portable across macOS + Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY by S1 (implementation; its AC4) + S6 (docs; its AC5). Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child (Scope "Out: snapshot/cache internals (S1/S2)"). Not an absence.
- AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY covered, SPLIT: THIS child OWNS the default-ref/source tunable half (its advisory AC: the ref/source defaults resolve via the standard rebar config + REBAR_* env precedence — named, not a deferred mechanism choice; its What "Defaults configurable" + the additional-notes config-precedence note); S2b (b73c AC9) owns the free-space watermark + grace/max-age windows; docs by S6. This child carries its half. Not an absence.
- AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — covered by S2/S2b (b73c AC10 cache/janitor architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat lease) + S4 (30b3 AC1 manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision in the ADR) + S4b (e308 AC3 ADR 0002). THIS child's What cross-references the ADR ("authored in S2, cross-referenced here") but carries no ADR-authoring AC and does not need one. Bucket STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence (S2/S2b/S4/S4b author the ADR clauses).
- AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children (this is S3 of 8) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC3/AC8. The "NEVER the server's mutable checkout" clause is PRECISELY this child's core deliverable: re-rooting the gate file tools / cfg.repo_path / pai_tools root onto the snapshot dir across all three dispatch touchpoints (its AC1/AC3), with the AC7 wrong-branch-invariance regression test as the behavioral proof that the false-negative this epic exists to kill cannot recur. The "client-specified" surfacing is S5; the "immutable/reproducible/pinned-SHA" basis is S1; the "signed/verified_at_sha/manifest channel" act is S4; the "unsigned local preserves verify-before-push" reader+back-out half is this child (AC4 + advisory AC), the signing-bar half is S4. No uncovered obligation introduced.

Single-ref code-review (parent Out-of-scope behavioral collapse) — covered at the BEHAVIOR level by THIS child (its AC5: code-review uses a single snapshot at a single ref, no base+head pair, like the others). Named-owner present; not an absence. (The SURFACE-side retirement of the pre-existing base/head/--diff-file controls is a standing G4 seam between S3 and S5 — a consistency concern about WHO retires the surface controls — not a G3 union-coverage gap; the single-ref code-review obligation is CLAIMED by a child here.)

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences): this child's Scope explicitly delegates signing rules to S4 (30b3), CLI/MCP surface schemas to S5 (b7fa), snapshot/cache internals to S1/S2 (6d56/70c7/b73c), and the architecture ADR (authored in S2, cross-referenced here). Each delegated obligation is claimed by a named sibling present in the roster. Covered-by-named-consumer.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) / S4b (e308) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4/S4b seam, where THIS child re-roots only "the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path)" and lists completion.py/pai_tools.py/mcp_server.py (NOT attest.py) in file_impact — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads the handle into the attestation hashing path, not a G3 union-coverage gap. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, and the ref-coherence clause now owned by S4b e308 AC1) remain CLAIMED by children, so the union covers them; the WHO-threads-it question lives in the G4 channel.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the gate-threading / re-rooting slice (ref+source on all five gates; attested reads the snapshot dir across all three dispatch touchpoints; local in-place read flagged unsigned + documented back-out; back-compat origin/main+attested defaults that are configurable via the standard rebar config + REBAR_* precedence; consumer absent/GC'd-snapshot tolerance; single-ref code-review; the wrong-branch-invariance regression test) of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of parent AC1's re-rooting half and the "reads in-place checkout" + "documented back-out" halves of AC3, and carries the default-ref half of AC7. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the eight-story roster. NO G3 finding for this pairing.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Plan PASS, stale only by HEAD movement; implementing S3 gate threading (S1 dep closed).".

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Threaded ref+source through all 5 code-reading gates via new gate_source.py + a context-local code root (contextvars) that LLMConfig.from_env honors, so configs rebuilt deep in the workflow (gate_ops citation resolution/reconcile) also read the pinned snapshot. attested re-roots reads to the snapshot; local reads the in-place checkout flagged unsigned (source/signable/verified_at_sha annotated). Defaults origin/main+attested via REBAR_GATE_*/[snapshot]. Regression test proves the attested verdict read-basis is identical regardless of checked-out branch. CLI close gate = source=local (single-dev verify-before-push); offline tests default local via conftest. Subagent review: no blocking issues, all 10 ACs met; folded findings (review_code ref defaults to head; contextvar deep-rebuild test; thread-caveat doc). 13 S3 + 681 gate/workflow tests green; ruff clean.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
**Completion verification: PASS** — all 11 acceptance criteria (7 primary + 4 advisory) are met by the implementation.

**AC1 (five gates accept ref+source; attested reads snapshot, never working tree):** All five gates (`review_plan`, `verify_completion`, `review_ticket`, `review_code`, `scan_spec`) accept `ref`/`source` and call `gate_source.resolve_gate_handle` + `gate_source.apply_handle` within `gate_read_root`. Citations: `plan_review/__init__.py:83–95`, `completion.py:195–210`, `operations.py:121–166`, `code_review.py:133–156`, `spec_scan.py:64–80`, `gate_source.py:106–111`.

**AC2 (defaults origin/main+attested, configurable):** `default_ref`/`default_source` in `gate_source.py:67–76` resolve via `REBAR_GATE_REF`/`REBAR_GATE_SOURCE` env → `[snapshot]` config table → documented defaults. Tests at `test_gate_source_threading.py:166–188` confirm env and config-file paths.

**AC3 (all three dispatch touchpoints consume the snapshot handle):** The `use_code_root` context-var (`config.py:44–68`) is set by `gate_read_root` for attested runs; `LLMConfig.from_env` checks `current_code_root()` first (`config.py:314–317`). `gate_ops.completion_reconcile` and `completion_precheck` both call `LLMConfig.from_env` (gate_ops.py:60,104) inside the active context; `operations._assemble_context` is called from within the context at gate_ops.py:76. Test `test_context_code_root_reroots_deep_from_env` (threading test:191–205) verifies the mechanism explicitly.

**AC4 (local mode: in-place checkout, unsigned, repo_root as read root):** `materialize(SOURCE_LOCAL)` returns `SnapshotHandle(path=repo_root, sha=None)` (repo_snapshot.py:519–525); `signable` property returns `False` when sha is None (repo_snapshot.py:197–200); `gate_read_root` skips the context-set for local (gate_source.py:99–103). Test `test_local_mode_reads_checkout_and_is_unsigned` (threading test:144–160) asserts `signable=False`, `verified_at_sha=None`, and `runner.captured_repo_path == repo`.

**AC5 (review_code uses single snapshot at single ref):** `effective_ref = ref if ref is not None else head` (code_review.py:139–140) — one ref for the snapshot. Comment at code_review.py:173–176 explicitly states the single-ref design. The diff computation uses `base..head` for diff text only, file reads go through the snapshot.

**AC6 (gate tolerates absent/GC'd snapshot by re-materializing):** `cache.acquire` re-materializes when the entry dir is absent (cache.py:186–200). Test `test_gate_rematerializes_absent_snapshot` (threading test:211–221) uses `shutil.rmtree` to simulate eviction and confirms re-materialization. `test_deleted_entry_self_heals` (test_snapshot_cache.py:181–189) also covers this.

**AC7 (regression test: attested verdict identical regardless of checked-out branch):** `test_attested_reads_pinned_ref_regardless_of_checkout_branch` and `test_attested_verdict_identical_across_checkout_branches` (threading test:93–138) set up a repo where origin/main has `sentinel.txt="from-main\n"` and the checked-out branch has `"from-other\n"`, and assert the gate reads origin/main's content in both checkout states — directly reproducing and proving the fix for the wrong-branch false-negative.

**Advisory ACs:** Defaults via REBAR_GATE_* + [snapshot] config (confirmed above). ENOENT test: `test_deleted_entry_self_heals` + `test_gate_rematerializes_absent_snapshot`. Local-mode unsigned test: `test_local_mode_reads_checkout_and_is_unsigned`. Rollback doc: `docs/adr/0005-snapshot-cache-architecture.md:98` + `gate_source.py:12–14` + `transition.py:235–240`.

CLI close gate (`transition.py:241`) passes `source="local"` to `verify_completion`, confirming the single-developer verify-before-push path. `conftest.py:202–215` defaults offline tests to `REBAR_GATE_SOURCE=local` so attested tests are explicitly opt-in.

compiled_state.description:
# Thread ref + source mode through the code-reading gates

## Why

Today the gates derive their code root from the MCP process cwd / `repo_root` and read its
mutable working tree (`cfg.repo_path`). They must instead read the snapshot materialized at
the client-specified ref, with a uniform single-`ref` interface across every operation.

## What

- Add `ref` (branch|tag|SHA, default `origin/main`) and `source` (`attested` default |
  `local`) parameters to: `review_plan`, `verify_completion`, `review_ticket`, `review_code`,
  `scan_spec`. Every operation takes ONE ref and gets ONE snapshot (code-review included; no
  base+head pair in this epic).
- Attested: call S1/S2 to obtain a snapshot dir at the pinned SHA; point the gate's file
  tools (`pai_tools` root / `cfg.repo_path`) at the snapshot dir instead of the server cwd.
- Local: read the server's in-place checkout directly (no materialization, no cache); mark
  the run as unsigned (S4 bars signing). In local mode `repo_root` IS the read root (the
  checkout); in attested mode `repo_root` only locates the object DB and the snapshot is the
  read root.
- Replace the implicit `repo_root`-derived code root with the snapshot handle across all three
  dispatch touchpoints: `gate_ops`, `completion.py`, `operations._assemble_context`.
- **Back-compat / expand-contract:** existing callers that pass neither param get the
  documented defaults (ref=`origin/main`, source=`attested`), so the change is additive; state
  this back-out path (defaults preserve today's "verify latest" intent without the wrong-branch bug).
- **Consumer tolerance:** a gate whose snapshot dir is absent/being retired (S2 GC) treats it
  as a miss and re-materializes (ENOENT -> re-acquire), never reads a partial tree.
- **Defaults configurable:** the `ref`/`source` defaults are overridable per deployment, not
  hardcoded constants.

## Scope

- In: parameter threading, gate file-tool re-rooting to the snapshot, local-mode in-place read,
  back-compat defaults, consumer absent/stale tolerance.
- Out: signing rules (S4); CLI/MCP surface schemas (S5); snapshot/cache internals (S1/S2);
  the architecture ADR (authored in S2, cross-referenced here).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the five gates accepts `ref` + `source`; attested reads the snapshot dir, never
      the server's working tree.
- [ ] Omitting the params applies the documented defaults (ref=`origin/main`, source=`attested`);
      the defaults are configurable, not hardcoded.
- [ ] All three dispatch touchpoints (`gate_ops`, `completion.py`,
      `operations._assemble_context`) consume the snapshot handle — not just the interface signature.
- [ ] `local` mode reads the in-place checkout (dirty allowed), is flagged unsigned, and uses
      `repo_root` as the read root; attested uses `repo_root` only to locate the object DB.
- [ ] code-review uses a single snapshot at a single ref (no base+head pair) like the others.
- [ ] A gate tolerates an absent/being-GC'd snapshot dir by re-materializing (ENOENT -> miss),
      never reading a partial tree.
- [ ] Regression test asserts the attested verdict is IDENTICAL regardless of the server's
      checked-out branch (the behavioral contract — NOT an output snapshot / change-detector),
      reproducing this session's wrong-branch false-negative.

## Additional notes + Acceptance Criteria (advisory remediation)

- Terminology: "snapshot dir" and "snapshot handle" refer to the SAME entity (the handle wraps
  the dir + SHA + cleanup); used interchangeably.
- Defaults config mechanism: `ref`/`source` defaults are read through the EXISTING rebar config
  precedence (rebar.toml `[llm]`/gate table + `REBAR_*` env override), consistent with how
  other LLM/gate settings resolve — not a bespoke new mechanism.
- Rollback: `source=local` is the explicit back-out that restores the prior in-place working-
  tree read if the default snapshot read ever regresses a deployment.

- [ ] The `ref`/`source` defaults resolve via the standard rebar config + `REBAR_*` env
      precedence (named, not a deferred mechanism choice).
- [ ] A test covers the ENOENT/absent-snapshot path (gate re-materializes, never reads a partial tree).
- [ ] A test covers local-mode unsigned flagging (a local run is reflected unsigned in the result).
- [ ] `source=local` is documented as the rollback to in-place reads.

file_impact.reason: context-local code root honored by from_env

compiled_state.title: Thread ref + source mode through the code-reading gates
